meaning: every 15 minutes during every other hour

slate.text: Cada 15 minutos, una hora sí y otra no.

A.note: Grammatically correct, but slightly ambiguous as it could be read as 'every 15 minutes for a duration of two hours'.

B.note: Same as A; the comma provides a slight pause but doesn't fully clarify the 'every other' logic.

C.note: The most natural way to express 'every other' in Spanish to avoid ambiguity.

D.note: Same as A; using the digit '2' is common in schedules but doesn't improve the phrasing.

meaning: every minute during every other hour

A.note: Correct, but 'horas alternas' sounds slightly formal or technical.

B.note: Very natural and clear for a schedule description.

C.note: Equally natural; using the digit is common in schedules.

D.note: Same as B, but the period is irrelevant per instructions.

meaning: every 15 minutes during every third hour

slate.text: Cada 15 minutos durante cada tercera hora.

A.note: Simple and clear, though slightly ambiguous as to whether it means 'every 3rd hour' or 'every 3 hours'.

B.note: Too literal a translation of the English structure; sounds robotic/unnatural in Spanish.

C.note: The comma helps separate the frequency from the interval, making it very clear.

D.note: Same as C; the use of digits or words for numbers doesn't significantly affect naturalness here.

meaning: every 20 minutes during the 9 a.m., 11 a.m., 1 p.m., 3 p.m., and 5 p.m. hours

slate.text: cada 20 minutos durante las horas de las 9, 11, 13, 15 y 17
Cada 20 minutos, cada 2 horas, entre las 09:00 y las 17:59
Cada 20 minutos durante las horas de las 9:00, 11:00, 13:00, 15:00 y 17:00.

A.note: Very natural and accurate; using 24h format is common for schedules in Spanish.

B.note: Incorrect; implies a continuous range rather than specific disjointed hours.

C.note: Ambiguous; 'a las 9' usually means exactly at 9:00, not throughout the 9th hour.

D.note: Correct and clear, though slightly more formal/stiff due to the full timestamps.

meaning: every minute during the 12 a.m., 5 a.m., 10 a.m., 3 p.m., and 8 p.m. hours

slate.text: cada minuto durante las horas de las 0, 5, 10, 15 y 20
Cada minuto durante las horas de las 12, 5, 10, 15 y 20.
Cada minuto durante las horas de las 00:00, 05:00, 10:00, 15:00 y 20:00.

A.note: Accurate and natural, using 24h format which is common for schedules.

B.note: Accurate; mixing 12 and 24h formats is slightly less consistent than A, but common.

C.note: Redundant to use full timestamps (00:00) when referring to the entire hour.

D.note: Incorrect meaning; implies a frequency of every 5 hours, not specific hours of the day.
